Lilly’s Foundayo reaches 1,390 patients in first week, trailing Novo’s oral Wegovy launch
snippet: While Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill reached more than 3,000 patients in its first week on the market, analysts at RBC Capital Markets said a direct comparison of the two figures could be misleading given the shorter data collection time for Foundayo.

title: UPDATE: Kailera breaks biotech IPO record with $625M debut
snippet: Obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics debuted on the Nasdaq Friday after raising a record $625 million, beating Moderna’s $600 million from 2018.

title: RFK Jr. defends Makary, claims pharma ‘owns’ Congress and media
snippet: The pharma industry “own Congress, they own the media,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. told lawmakers by way of explaining the bad press against FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the second rejection of Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug.

title: Novo may have muscle advantage over Lilly in weight-loss race: preprint
snippet: More patients on Eli Lilly’s tirzepatide lost over 5% of their lean mass versus those on Novo Nordisk’s semaglutide, according to a study that has yet to be peer reviewed.

title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. title: Merck’s PD-1/VEGF data star in stacked lineup of AACR ‘26 data reveals
snippet: The Merck update, which will shed light on a $588 million bet to succeed Keytruda, is part of a roster of presentations that could shape the future of ADCs, protein degraders and KRAS-targeted therapies.

Discovery of an Endonuclease G-inhibitory Ku80-peptide protecting against leukemogenic rearrangements at the MLL breakpoint cluster.
snippet: Endonuclease G (EndoG) is an evolutionarily conserved enzyme that cleaves the Mixed Lineage Leukemia breakpoint cluster region (MLLbcr) under sublethal chemotherapeutic treatment conditions, causing leukemogenic chromosomal rearrangements. While endogenous inhibitors (EndoGI) control EndoG in lower organisms, no such EndoGI has been identified in mammalian....

title: External validation of PREDICT Breast v3.1 for overall survival in international cohorts, including young and invasive lobular subgroups.
snippet: PREDICT Breast is an online tool that provides survival predictions for patients with early-stage breast cancer, for different treatments after surgery. External validation is essential to assess model performance across populations and healthcare settings. We aimed to externally validate PREDICT using clinical practice data from the Netherlands, Sweden, an...

title: In vivo exposure to CXCL12-high CAFs and chronic CXCL12 stimulation in vitro is sufficient to enhance metastasis of ER-positive breast cancer.
snippet: Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) play important roles in breast cancer (BC) progression and metastasis. Here we investigated whether CAFs from indolent vs. aggressive BCs differ in gene expression profiles and how they impact metastasis. Genotypic differences in CAF lines from basal-like (CAF23) and luminal-A BC (CAF19), were compared and effects on CAF...

title: Single-cell RNA sequencing and Mendelian randomization identify context-dependent LIPA-associated classical monocyte states in sepsis and breast cancer.
snippet: Breast cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related death in women, and sepsis is a major complication in cancer patients that worsens prognosis. Classical monocytes and the lysosomal acid lipase gene LIPA have been implicated in immune regulation, but their roles across these two disease contexts remain incompletely understood.
